headline: US software stocks rebound, seeking to loosen AI's grip
summary: Shares of several U.S. software stocks gained on Tuesday, as the industry attempts a comeback after being battered for much of ‌the year on fears of disruption from artificial intelligence.  The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF hit its highest level since January before paring gains, while Workday, ServiceNow and Salesforce rose between 1.4% and 2.4%.  The gains hint at a possible shift in investor sentiment as markets reassess software stocks following a painful valuation ⁠reset.

raw.headline: US software stocks rebound, seeking to loosen AI's grip

The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF hit its highest level since January before paring gains, while Workday, ServiceNow and Salesforce rose between 1.4% and 2.4%.  The gains hint at a possible shift in investor sentiment as markets reassess software stocks following a painful valuation ⁠reset.

headline: JAGGAER Appoints Procurement Leader Craig Cuffie to Board of Directors
summary: RESEARCH TRIANGLE PARK, N.C., May 19, 2026--JAGGAER, a global leader in AI-powered source-to-pay and supplier collaboration software, today announced the appointment of Craig Cuffie to its Board of Directors. The move strengthens JAGGAER’s ability to help enterprises navigate growing supply chain complexity and make faster, more intelligent spend decisions.

headline: How Investors Are Reacting To Salesforce (CRM) After Bank of America Flags AI Monetization Challenges
summary: In mid-May 2026, Bank of America reinstated coverage of Salesforce with an Underperform rating, arguing that the company faces a structural reset as AI reshapes its core CRM and agentic offerings. The bank’s critique that Salesforce may be shifting from a high-growth story to a mature cash generator highlights a tension between its aggressive AI investments and questions over how effectively those tools will be monetized. We’ll now examine how Bank of America’s skepticism about Salesforce’s...
